item.title: Investor support for executive pay rises even as awards rocket
item.description: Shareholders tend to vote for high compensation as long as it is linked to performance

item.title: Losers in ‘creditor-on-creditor violence’ left with crumbs
item.description: Moody’s analysis of adversarial corporate debt exchanges shows losers recover just 14 cents on the dollar

item.title: Elliott presses Bunzl for buybacks after building stake
item.description: Business supplies distributor is latest in string of UK targets for US activist hedge fund

item.title: Social media ban reveals Starmer’s coherence problem
item.description: If 16-year-olds are old enough to vote, they should have access to the same information environment as adults

item.title: Ban on TikTok and YouTube will make children ‘happier’, says Starmer
item.description: PM announces that he expects ban on under-16s using some platforms to be in place early next year

item.title: Starbucks Korea to close stores for staff training after ‘Tank Day’ scandal
item.description: Coffee chain’s owner says move ‘shows how seriously we are taking the marketing incident’

item.title: China summons Walmart chain Sam’s Club over food safety issues
item.description: US retailer has expanded rapidly in world’s second-largest economy

item.title: The spectacle of SpaceX trumps DCF models
item.description: The public focused on the mission, not the multiple

item.title: Sweden demands other EU states tackle Russian oil tankers in their waters
item.description: Also in this newsletter: Kallas fronts up amid EEAS reform talk

item.title: How SpaceX hit its $2tn valuation
item.description: Plus, end of an era for Asia’s slickest brokerage, the US stock supply drought is over, and some striking Kapoor in London

item.title: FirstFT: Iran and US agree Hormuz deal
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Ukraine’s drone campaign and BBC job cuts

item.title: Europe’s Climate Leaders 2026: interactive listing
item.description: Annual list highlights companies making progress on cutting carbon emissions*

item.title: Europe’s Climate Leaders
item.description: EU climate policy rollback highlights challenge of green transition, with early movers facing uncertainty. Plus: AI promises efficiency gains, but at a cost; and the annual FT-Statista list of companies making progress in cutting carbon emissions

item.title: Milanese magic: how UniCredit won support for its lowball Commerzbank bid
item.description: Tender offer tactics used by investment banker Andrea Orcel deliver latest twist in 21-month takeover battle

item.title: BBC news teams to lose hundreds of jobs in radical downsizing
item.description: Cuts likely to affect specific radio shows as part of corporation plans to reduce costs

item.title: Lessons from Ireland’s Revolut revolution
item.description: The fintech’s model is proving more suitable for international scaling than the often outdated systems of legacy rivals

item.title: SFR’s €20bn break-up can win watchdog approval, says Bouygues
item.description: Lead bidder for French telecoms operator hopes EU’s competitive drive will offset regulatory concerns about merger

item.title: Legal AI start-up Legora to double headcount
item.description: Company with $5.6bn valuation has seen 900% more website visits since its Jude Law-fronted campaign

item.title: Asian stocks soar with ‘risk on’ rally after US-Iran peace deal
item.description: Investor euphoria following Friday’s record SpaceX IPO helps propel Japanese and Korean markets

item.title: Can Blue Origin escape the shadow of SpaceX?
item.description: Jeff Bezos’s rocket company is rebuilding just as its bigger rival reaps $75bn from a stratospheric IPO

item.title: eToro exploring acquisitions amid push into traditional banking services
item.description: Digital trading platform could apply for banking licence

item.title: Call for entries: UK’s leading management consultants 2027
item.description: Clients and peers are invited to rate advisory firms on their expertise

item.title: ‘Time kills a deal’ more than ever in a slow housing market
item.description: In prime London, the ratio of homes under offer to fall-throughs has not been this high since the 2008 financial crisis. Imminent conveyancing reform needs to be radical

item.title: Revival of interest in nuclear power gives hope to start-ups
item.description: Investment in both fission and fusion suggests attitudes are changing

item.title: AI efficiency gains come at a high energy cost
item.description: Technology is tackling complexities that have held back progress in cutting energy waste

item.title: Energy shocks push Italian businesses to decarbonise
item.description: Companies are pressing ahead with emissions cuts to boost efficiency and competitiveness

item.title: AI holds the key to faster battery tech development
item.description: Opportunity to transform materials discovery could outweigh risks of high energy consumption

item.title: First movers risk losing out in EU green policy pullback
item.description: Companies that acted early to decarbonise now face uncertainty as Brussels waters down climate targets

item.title: Singapore to launch gold clearing system in bid to become precious metals hub
item.description: City-state seeks to shake up trade dominated by London, New York and Switzerland

item.title: Finance students’ ‘near-peer’ mentoring raises school pupils’ financial literacy
item.description: An FT survey found growth in business school outreach programmes — and calls for more to contribute
